story_summary: A personal testimony recounting lifelong Christian formation beginning with childhood VBS, a pivotal conversion at age eleven, sustained service in church leadership, exploration of other faiths, and a conviction of stewardship and gratitude.

context.stakes: spiritual identity, family relationships, stewardship of church resources, community leadership

core_conflict: How to live out, sustain, and express a lifelong Christian commitment through personal choices, family decisions, and public leadership responsibilities.

decision_moments.trigger: an intense week of Bible drills, scripture memorization and counsel at Vacation Bible School in 1952
constraints: father was not a Christian and initially ill-prepared to counsel
parents felt ill prepared for the discussion
options: not profess faith at that time
decision_moments.choice: prayed to receive Christ and was baptized the following week (alongside his father)
decision_moments.reasoning: He felt understood and special after counseling by Brother Cutrell and perceived the moment as life-altering and definitive — "No turning back."
decision_moments.outcome: "We were baptized together the following week."

decision_moments.trigger: a perceived obligation of stewardship and ongoing commitment to support the church and community
constraints: time and resource demands of leadership
options: take on leadership responsibilities (e.g., chair committees, chair capital campaign)
decision_moments.choice: chaired committees and a capital campaign
decision_moments.reasoning: A conviction of stewardship and obligation to go the extra mile in supporting church and community motivated accepting responsibility.
decision_moments.risk_accepted: accepting responsibility for major fundraising and organizational effort
decision_moments.outcome: "I chaired a successful capital campaign that raised over $16 million for new facilities."

decision_moments.trigger: curiosity about other religions encountered through travel and personal encounters (e.g., Mormon missionary, Buddhist temple visit)
decision_moments.choice: read the Book of Mormon cover to cover and engaged in dialogue with a Buddhist monk; studied comparative religion
decision_moments.reasoning: Intellectual curiosity and desire to understand other points of view and dispel misconceptions about other faiths.
decision_moments.risk_accepted: exposure to differing beliefs while remaining committed to Christian fundamentals
decision_moments.outcome: "All of this has enriched my understanding of the world’s religions and made me more respectful of different points of view."

principles.principle: Spiritual formation is rooted in early, repeated practices and rituals.
principles.evidence: The fundamentals have not changed. I just believe I understand them a little better.

principles.principle: Mentorship and pastoral presence can catalyze decisive commitments.
principles.evidence: It was through his tutelage and leadership that I began to take spirituality seriously.

principles.principle: Stewardship entails both time and money as obligations to community and faith.
principles.evidence: we have a conviction that we have a stewardship responsibility to others.

principles.principle: Active participation in religious programs deepens faith and personal growth.
principles.evidence: Every single one of these activities has enriched my life and served to enhance my faith.
The basic story of my spiritual experience is one of gratitude.

decision_heuristics.heuristic: When a trusted spiritual leader engages, allow space for counsel and follow through on commitments.
decision_heuristics.evidence: He seemed to understand me. He made me feel so special that day

decision_heuristics.heuristic: Solidify pivotal commitments through public ritual to mark and sustain the decision.
decision_heuristics.evidence: We were baptized together the following week.

decision_heuristics.heuristic: Accept leadership responsibilities when motivated by stewardship and ability to contribute time and resources.
decision_heuristics.evidence: we have a conviction that we have a stewardship responsibility to others.

decision_heuristics.heuristic: Learn about other faiths through direct reading and conversation to increase respect and correct misconceptions.
decision_heuristics.evidence: I read the entire book, cover to cover

leadership_patterns.risk_tolerance: moderate — willing to accept responsibility for significant community initiatives but grounded in stewardship and tradition
leadership_patterns.communication_style: personal, testimonial, relational — uses stories and mentor references to influence
leadership_patterns.accountability_posture: stewardship-oriented and service-driven; accepts public responsibility (e.g., campaign chair)
leadership_patterns.ethical_framing: faith-based duty and gratitude; decisions framed as obligations to God, family, and community

quotes.quote: T o God be the glory.
We were baptized together the following week.
we have a conviction that we have a stewardship responsibility to others.
Every single one of these activities has enriched my life and served to enhance my faith.
